transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-08-05]
[00:00] You: Security audit planning meeting. We have a third-party pen test scheduled for September 15th.
[00:50] Speaker 1: Before the pen test, I need to do a full inventory of our attack surface. I'll complete the asset inventory by August 18th.
[01:25] Speaker 2: I'll run an internal vulnerability scan using Nessus and address any critical findings by August 22nd.
[02:00] Speaker 3: The pen test scope needs to be agreed with the vendor. I'll finalize the scope of work document and send it to them by August 10th.
[02:35] Speaker 4: We should also make sure our incident response runbook is updated. I'll review and update the IR runbook by August 15th.
[03:10] You: Good. What about dependency vulnerabilities?
[03:25] Speaker 1: I'll audit our npm and pip dependencies for known CVEs and upgrade anything with a high or critical severity. I'll have that done by August 20th.
[04:00] Speaker 2: I should also check our secret management — we've had a few cases where API keys ended up in logs. I'll audit the logging config and ensure no secrets are ever written to logs. I'll fix any issues by August 19th.
[04:40] Speaker 3: For the pen test prep, I'll set up a dedicated test environment that mirrors production. I'll have it ready by September 8th.
[05:10] Speaker 4: I'll also coordinate with the pen testing vendor to confirm logistics — start time, access credentials, emergency contacts. I'll confirm all of that by September 10th.
[05:45] You: Great. Post-pen-test debrief will be September 30th. We'll review findings and prioritize remediations.

decisions: Third-party pen test scheduled for September 15th
